title: Async Context, Logs, Metrics, Traces, Health, and Incident Evidence · Contract Lens: Liveness Readiness Startup
summary: The service logs secrets and raw paths, labels metrics by identifiers, loses trace context, reports healthy during saturation, and cannot connect an alert to a user outcome. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts liveness readiness startup increment in bounded telemetry and health evidence contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate liveness, readiness, startup, dependency state, saturation, draining, and management endpoint protection without causing probe storms.
Create OpenTelemetry server spans with stable route names, span kind, method, status, error, network, server, propagation, sampling, and sensitive-data policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Liveness Readiness Startup · Theory Liveness Readiness Startup
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 1641 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to emit bounded route telemetry and distinguish server errors from readiness capacity; prove cleanup before the service deadline expires; then saturate one tenant budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Liveness Readiness Startup contract for the supplied data.
